children.text: The Human Layer: the missing layer above the tech stack - trust, coordination, and shared understanding - so humanity and technology can advance in harmony.

detailBody.alt: The Human Layer — trust, coordination, and shared understanding in healthcare AI

children.text: As AI becomes embedded in healthcare products, the compliance stakes have never been higher. This event brings together compliance leaders, startup operators, and senior executives on one stage to cut through the noise — offering honest, experience-backed perspectives on what it actually takes to build in this regulated space.
Expect candid panel discussions, real-world examples, and a strong emphasis on AI governance and data security — the areas where healthtech teams most often underestimate their exposure.
What you'll gain
Live panel discussions: Real conversations on compliance strategy, AI risk, and security obligations in healthtech
Practical guidelines & tips: Actionable frameworks you can apply immediately — not theory, but ground-level advice
Exclusive Google Drive resource pack: Curated compliance guidelines and templates shared post-event — a lasting reference for your team
Deep dive: AI & security: A dedicated focus on AI-specific compliance risks and how to build secure, trustworthy health products
Our Expert Panelists:
Our discussion features leaders who are actively shaping the future of healthtech security:
Panelists:
Megan Kane – Executive Director of Rellia, specializing in AI-enabled SaMD, diagnostics, and global regulatory strategy for FDA, Health Canada, EU MDR, and APAC compliance.

Andre Padure – Head of Regulatory Affairs and Quality Assurance at RetiSpec, leading global regulatory strategy and quality systems for the company’s AI-powered retinal imaging platform for early Alzheimer’s disease detection.
Roy Kirshon – COO & Co-Founder of RetiSpec, leading company strategy, financing, and operations to support the development and commercialization of healthcare technologies.
Moderator:
Katie Duyen Nguyen - Regional Director of BD @ CyStack
Brought to you by:

RetiSpec - A Toronto-based medical AI company using advanced imaging technology to detect signs of neurodegenerative disease - including Alzheimer's - through a simple, non-invasive retinal scan. RetiSpec's clinically validated AI analyzes retinal images captured by standard eye clinic cameras to identify disease biomarkers before symptoms appear, putting powerful early detection tools directly at the point of care.

Rellia Health - A community that connects promising digital health founders with industry experts, healthcare practitioners, and engaged investors. Rellia is a network of people who deeply understand the healthcare industry and will go out of their way to help you succeed. We connect early-stage digital health, medical device, wellness, and diagnostic companies with the personalized solutions that match their unique needs.

CyStack - Blends deep offensive security knowledge with proprietary tooling to help organizations protect their products, data, and operations. Their suite covers penetration testing, automated vulnerability scanning (VulnScan), crowdsourced bug bounty (WhiteHub), secrets management (Locker), and 24/7 security monitoring — giving healthtech startups everything they need to build secure from day one.

“Toronto Tech Week is a citywide celebration of the people building what’s next. From May 26–29, 2026, founders, investors, and builders come together for hundreds of community-led events across Toronto, connecting tens of thousands of people around Canadian tech.”
Torontotechweek.com
Please be advised

Unfortunately, space is very limited at these community events and we can not always accept everyone we would like to. If you are not accepted to this event, please keep applying! We appreciate your application tremendously and we are looking forward to seeing you at a future event very soon!

title: AI Healthcare Compliance (w/ The AI Collective)

children.text: Founders and Investors often operate under intense responsibility and visibility.

Join Dr Sabina Nagpal for this interactive, neuroscience-informed session which focuses on maintaining judgment, clarity, and presence when navigating complex decisions, competing priorities and high-pressure situations.
Founders and Investors often operate under intense responsibility and visibility.

Join Dr Sabina Nagpal for this interactive, neuroscience-informed session which focuses on maintaining judgment, clarity, and presence when navigating complex decisions, competing priorities and high-pressure situations.
A practical session for clinicians and founders focused on primary care workflows, adoption signals, and what actually gets used.
Bring your questions. We’ll cover common blockers, simple pilot design, and how to turn frontline feedback into product decisions.
A practical session for clinicians and founders focused on primary care workflows, adoption signals, and what actually gets used.
Bring your questions. We’ll cover common blockers, simple pilot design, and how to turn frontline feedback into product decisions.

Eric Haywood has been on both sides of the investment table. As a four-time founder and investor at InterSystems Ventures, he has built companies, raised capital, and evaluated startups for investment. In this session, he will share what makes a company fundable and how to show up more prepared.
What you will walk away with:
What VCs look for in early-stage health tech companies
What signals separate successful companies from the rest
How to tell your story in a way that resonates with investors
Common mistakes founders make in the fundraising process and how to avoid them
This is a practical, no-fluff session for founders who are serious about fundraising and want an honest look at how the process works.
About Us:
Rellia is a virtual incubator connecting digital health founders with investors, clinicians, and advisors to launch healthcare solutions. https://www.relliahealth.com/

title: Investor Readiness: How VCs Evaluate Startups

children.text: You've built something that works. So why are you struggling to make the sale?
The problem is that healthcare buyers have a different definition of "ready to purchase" than most founders expect.
This webinar will help you get in the door.
What you'll walk away with:
How to revise your pitch for what healthcare buyers actually care about
How to turn your model card into your most persuasive sales asset
What AI regulations actually mean for your product and timeline
How to align your positioning with health system priorities instead of only selling your features
Your speaker:

Brenton Hill, Head of Operations and General Counsel at the Coalition for Health AI (CHAI). Before CHAI, Brenton spent years at Mayo Clinic Platform doing exactly what health system buyers do: evaluating AI vendors, assessing regulatory risk, and deciding what gets purchased.
About CHAI:

The nonprofit setting the gold standard for responsible AI in healthcare, representing 3,000+ organizations across health systems, academia, and industry. Their frameworks are increasingly what buyers reference when evaluating AI vendors.

title: Why Healthcare Keeps Saying No to Your AI (And How to Fix It)

children.text: Whether you are building a quality management system for the first time or trying to improve the QMS you already have, this session is designed to help you answer your questions.
Join this live 1:1 session with quality experts who have supported medical device teams through writing SOPs, passing certification audits, and securing regulatory approvals.
This is a safe space to ask questions and get guidance on how to build the right-sized QMS processes for your company.
The quality reviewers bring knowledge and experience in:
21 CFR Part 820
MDSAP
MDR/IVDR and more
Building or updating your QMS does not have to feel overwhelming. Our experts are excited to help you.

This event is for clinicians and founders to meet and share ideas to make healthcare technology better.

For healthcare professionals, this is a chance to share your expertise on what works and what doesn't. We know you're tired of being told to adopt technology that doesn't function the way it should. Your feedback can make a major impact for founders, while also opening potential avenues for advisory or leadership opportunities in the tech industry.

For founders, this is a unique opportunity to gain 1:1 feedback directly from interested clinicians. Understanding how your technology will fit into real workflows will help strengthen your product's design and adoption.
These sessions can open doors to:
Co-developing solutions based on real unmet needs
Filling advisory board member, co-founder, or chief medical officer roles
Discussing feedback to help make innovations more practical and effective in clinical settings
Exploring pilot programs, validation studies, or new business opportunities

Like everything we do at Rellia and Thrive MD Connect, this event is about genuine connection. There are no obligations to commit to anything, but you may find someone here who changes the course of your career for the better.

This session explores the commercialization journey for health startups, focusing on how to move beyond the prototype stage and build a purposeful business strategy that connects your idea to your customer's needs.

Drawing on real-world expertise, we'll cover the critical elements of funding and business strategy from prototype to scale, and why understanding the foundations of your business creates the path for success.
Attendees will leave with actionable insights on how to avoid common pitfalls, position their product for adoption, and scale with intention in the competitive health innovation landscape.

title: Scaling with Purpose: From Prototype to Customer in Health Startups

children.text: Most digital health founders lead with technology, but growth starts with trust. Beyond the Product helps you turn complex science into a clear, credible brand that connects with the people who matter most—investors, partners, and patients.
Rellia has partnered with Brave Tale to bring you a workshop for translating your technical product features into a compelling brand story.

title: Beyond the Product: How digital health brands earn trust and drive growth

children.text: Rellia members get exclusive access to 1:1 pitch opportunities with Forum Ventures, a leading early-stage fund for B2B SaaS founders.
No crowded competitions or flashy events—just direct conversations about what you're building with people who want to help you grow.
Forum has backed 500+ companies who went on to raise over $1B in follow-on funding. Now they're looking at healthcare startups innovating in:
Staff management: tackling shortages and burnout with tools that improve retention, create flexible staffing, or offload manual work.
Operational efficiency: reducing patient length of stay, preventing denials, and streamlining communication through AI and automation.
Access to care: hybrid and virtual models that expand reach, improve patient engagement, and tie directly to outcomes in risk-based contracts.
This is your chance to connect with investors who are actively writing checks and genuinely excited about healthcare innovation.

AI ethics is often framed as a technical checklist—fairness, transparency, accountability. But the truth is, the biggest risks in AI don't come from the machines. They come from us. From the assumptions we code in, the decisions we greenlight, and the people we leave out of the room.

Join Argentina Beltran, founder of InclusifAI and author of What We Teach the Machines, for a provocative and honest talk on the human side of AI ethics. Through real-world case studies and personal storytelling, she'll challenge us to see ethics not as an abstract framework, but as a mirror—asking what we're teaching the machines, and whether we're willing to learn from ourselves.

Hosted by Rellia Health and StartUp Lab, this session is for builders, funders, and anyone shaping AI systems who wants to move beyond buzzwords toward responsibility with depth, courage, and humanity.

We are official event hosts with Waterloo Tech Week 2025, a celebration of what's been built here, and what's still to come. We're building something great together. September 8-11, 2025. waterlootechweek.ca

title: Ethics in AI: Less About Tech, More About Humans

children.text: You're building healthcare products that need to work for real patients and providers, but how do you know if you're on the right track?
Join a seasoned UX researcher with 20 years of experience in health tech to see how user research actually works in practice.

We'll start with a real case study from a healthcare startup that used research to dramatically improve their onboarding—and the impressive numbers that followed. Then we'll break down the decisions behind the research: how they chose the right methods, found the right participants, and turned insights into action.
Walk away understanding when research makes sense for your startup and when it's time to bring in expert help.

title: Second Opinion: When Healthcare Startups Need User Research
